title: Validate Object Parameter Before Loading Detail Page
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the application to validate the Salesforce object name in the URL before loading the detail page, so that I am not presented with empty or broken views for invalid objects.
description:
The Migration and Dictionary detail pages accept a Salesforce object name as a URL parameter. Without validation, users can navigate to detail pages for invalid or non-configured objects, resulting in empty screens.

This validation enforces three rules:
1. The object name must exist as a valid Salesforce object.
2. The object must already be in the user's configured data model (scorecard).
3. If a valid Salesforce object is not yet in the user's scorecard, a confirmation modal offers to add it.

If the object is completely invalid, the user is redirected to the scorecard page. The URL parameter is also sanitized before use in SQL queries to prevent injection.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid or missing object parameter redirects user to the scorecard page
Valid Salesforce object not in user's data model triggers a confirmation modal: '{ObjectName} is not currently included in your scorecard. Would you like to add it?'
Clicking 'Yes' on the modal adds the object to the user's scorecard and loads the detail page
Clicking 'Cancel' on the modal redirects the user to the scorecard page
If the object name is not a valid Salesforce object at all, an error message is shown and user is redirected
Object dropdown on the detail page only lists objects in the user's configured data model
URL parameter is sanitized before use in any SQL queries
ux_changes: Adds a confirmation modal when navigating to a valid but non-configured object. The modal offers 'Yes' to add the object or 'Cancel' to return to the scorecard. Object dropdown is filtered to only show configured objects.
middleware_changes: Server-side validation checks the URL object parameter against the user's configured data model and the Salesforce object list. Handles the add-to-scorecard flow via AJAX.
api_changes: AJAX endpoint to add a Salesforce object to the user's scorecard. Validates the object exists in Salesforce before committing.
implementation_notes: Validation checks occur on page load: first against the local data model, then against the Salesforce object list via API. SQL injection prevention is applied to the URL parameter before any database queries.
comment_summary: Developer implemented a three-tier validation: checks local DB first, then validates against Salesforce object list on 'Yes' confirmation, and shows an error if the object does not exist in Salesforce at all.
